title: OER ingest under the bounded-access protocol (metadata + short evidence)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. OER is referenced at source, never rehosted; ingest captures only metadata and short evidence under a bounded-access protocol.
objective: Build OER ingest that captures metadata and short evidence excerpts under the bounded-access protocol without rehosting OER corpora.
acceptanceCriteria: Ingests OER metadata and short evidence excerpts only under the bounded-access protocol; does NOT rehost or mirror full OER corpora.
Records provenance (retrievedAt, version, source url) and respects each source's access terms.
Short evidence excerpts stay within fair-use limits and only where framework/OER terms permit reproduction.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; the commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
